channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
item.description: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Broken VECT 2.0 ransomware acts as a data wiper for large files
item.description: Researchers are warning that the VECT 2.0 ransomware has a problem in the way it handles encryption nonces that leads to permanently destroying larger files rather than encrypt them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers are exploiting a critical LiteLLM pre-auth SQLi flaw
item.description: Hackers are targeting sensitive information stored in the LiteLLM open-source large-language model (LLM) gateway by exploiting a critical vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-42208. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NSA Chief During Snowden Affair Shares Regrets, Reflections 13 Years Later
item.description: Chris Inglis was the head civilian in charge at the NSA when the Snowden leak exploded. He gets candid about mistakes the organization made, and what CISOs need to know about spotting potential threat...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Feuding Ransomware Groups Leak Each Other's Data
item.description: When 0APT and KryBit attacked each other, they exposed infrastructure and operational data, giving defenders rare insight into ransomware operations....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Vidar Rises to Top of Chaotic Infostealer Market
item.description: The malware has filled the gap created by last year's law enforcement takedowns of Lumma and Rhadamanthys....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Video service Vimeo confirms Anodot breach exposed user data
item.description: Vimeo has disclosed that data belonging to some of its customers and users has been accessed without authorization following the recent breach at the Anodot data anomaly detection company. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Researchers Discover Critical GitHub CVE-2026-3854 RCE Flaw Exploitable via Single Git Push
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security vulnerability impacting GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server that could allow an authenticated user to obtain remote code exe...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Brazilian LofyGang Resurfaces After Three Years With Minecraft LofyStealer Campaign
item.description: A cybercrime group of Brazilian origin has resurfaced after more than three years to orchestrate a campaign that targets Minecraft players with a new stealer called LofyStealer (aka GrabBot). "The mal...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US reportedly charges Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland
item.description: A 19-year-old dual United States and Estonian citizen arrested in Finland earlier this month faces federal charges in the U.S. alleging he was a prolific member of the notorious Scattered Spider hacki...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fresh Wave of GlassWorm VS Code Extensions Slices Through Supply Chain
item.description: Attackers continue to scale a campaign to seed Open VSX with seemingly benign VS Code extensions that spread self-propagating malware....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Checkmarx confirms LAPSUS$ hackers leaked its stolen GitHub data
item.description: Application security company Checkmarx has confirmed that the LAPSUS$ threat group leaked data stolen from its private GitHub repository. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Irreversibly Destroys Files Over 131KB on Windows, Linux, ESXi
item.description: Threat hunters are warning that the cybercriminal operation known as VECT 2.0 acts more like a wiper than a ransomware due to a critical flaw in its encryption implementation across Windows, Linux, an...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft to deprecate legacy TLS in Exchange Online starting July
item.description: Microsoft says it will start blocking legacy TLS connections for POP and IMAP email clients in Exchange Online starting in July 2026. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Inside an OPSEC Playbook: How Threat Actors Evade Detection
item.description: Threat actors are now publishing structured OPSEC playbooks to stay undetected. Flare reveals how these guides outline layered infrastructure, identity separation, and long-term evasion strategies. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
item.description: Every security program is betting on the same assumption: once a system is connected, the problem is solved. Open a ticket, stand up a gateway, push the data through. Done. That assumption is wrong. I...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical Unpatched Flaw Leaves Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security flaw impacting LeRobot, Hugging Face's open-source robotics platform with nearly 24,000 GitHub stars, that could be exploited to...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: After Mythos: New Playbooks For a Zero-Window Era
item.description: When patching isn’t fast enough, NDR helps contain the next era of threats. If you’ve been tracking advancements in AI, you know the exploit window, the short buffer that organizations relied on to pa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft: New Remote Desktop warnings may display incorrectly
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed a new issue causing newly introduced Windows security warnings to display incorrectly when opening Remote Desktop (.rdp) files. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft asks iPhone users to reauthenticate after Outlook outage
item.description: After addressing a widespread outage that affected Outlook.com users worldwide on Monday, Microsoft has asked iPhone users to re-enter their credentials to regain access to their Outlook and Hotmail a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
item.description: A Chinese national accused of being a member of the Silk Typhoon hacking group has been extradited to the U.S. from Italy. Xu Zewei, 34, was arrested in July 2025 by Italian authorities for his alleg...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
item.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from Silverfor...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
item.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in que...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Robinhood account creation flaw abused to send phishing emails
item.description: Online trading platform Robinhood's account creation process was exploited by threat actors to inject phishing messages into legitimate emails, tricking users into believing their accounts had suspici...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GlassWorm malware attacks return via 73 OpenVSX "sleeper" extensions
item.description: A new wave of the Glassworm campaign is targeting the OpenVSX ecosystem with 73 "sleeper" extensions that turn malicious after an update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
item.description: A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom "Snow" malware in a multipronged campaign....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Canada arrests three for operating “SMS blaster” device in Toronto
item.description: Canadian authorities have arrested three men for operating an "SMS blaster" device that pretends to be a cellular tower to send phishing texts to nearby phones. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Alleged Silk Typhoon hacker extradited to US for cyberespionage
item.description: A Chinese national accused of carrying out cyberespionage operations for China's intelligence services has been extradited from Italy to the United States to face criminal charges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FTC: Americans lost over $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) warned of a massive increase in losses from social media scams since 2020, exceeding $2.1 billion in 2025. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
